rag.description: The WriteHost plugin prints informational messages to the console during automation execution by taking a provided argument and writing it to the host output stream. It helps with real-time progress visibility and troubleshooting without requiring log level configuration, and surfaces exceptions to preserve workflow visibility.

qa.question: What is the WriteHost plugin and why does it matter?
qa.answer: The WriteHost plugin prints informational messages to the console during automation runs so users can follow execution in real time and diagnose issues faster.

qa.question: What are the key features and primary parameters of this plugin?
qa.answer: Key features include writing the content of the Argument property to the host/console output. The primary parameter is Argument, which specifies the text or expression to print.

qa.question: How does this plugin integrate into automation workflows?
qa.answer: Under context.integration.sequentialWorkflow it is defined as an ActionRuleModel task with a console-oriented icon, making it available as a simple output/logging action in workflows.

qa.question: What are best practices for configuring and using this plugin?
qa.answer: Use clear, descriptive messages in Argument, include dynamic values via expressions when needed, and rely on these messages to trace execution flow during debugging.

qa.question: What is the structure of its rule definition?
qa.answer: Each rule uses "$type": "Action", includes pluginName "WriteHost", and provides a string or expression under the argument property.

qa.question: How does the plugin handle errors during execution?
qa.answer: If an error occurs, the exception is surfaced through the workflow output/log stream so it is visible during execution; workflow behavior (continue/stop) depends on the runner’s failure handling.

qa.question: Who is the author of this plugin?
qa.answer: The author is Roei Sabag, linked at https://www.linkedin.com/in/roei-sabag-247aa18/.

qa.question: What categories is this plugin listed under?
qa.answer: It belongs to the Logging category, since it provides visible runtime output useful for tracing execution.

qa.question: What GitHub documentation link is provided?
qa.answer: The GitHub documentation is available at https://github.com/g4-api/g4-plugins/blob/main/docs/Actions/WriteHost.md.

qa.question: What GitHub source link is provided?
qa.answer: The GitHub source code is available at https://github.com/g4-api/g4-plugins/blob/main/src/G4.Plugins.Common/Actions/WriteHost.cs.

qa.question: How is this plugin defined in the integration context?
qa.answer: Under context.integration.sequentialWorkflow it is defined with "$type": "Action", componentType "task", iconProvider "terminal", and model "ActionRuleModel".

qa.question: What is described in the description section?
qa.answer: The description explains that WriteHost prints informational messages during automation execution for immediate visibility, highlights progress tracking and debugging value, and lists usage in RPA and automation testing.

qa.question: What examples are provided in the manifest?
qa.answer: Examples include printing a static console message and printing a dynamic session-scoped parameter value, demonstrating both fixed and expression-based output.

qa.question: What is the key for this plugin?
qa.answer: The key is "WriteHost", which uniquely identifies this plugin in the system.

qa.question: What is the manifestVersion?
qa.answer: The manifestVersion is 4, indicating the schema version used for this plugin.

qa.question: Which platforms does this plugin support?
qa.answer: It supports Any platform, making it usable across different operating systems and environments.

qa.question: What is the pluginType?
qa.answer: The pluginType is Action, meaning it performs an operation within the automation workflow.

qa.question: What properties define how the plugin operates?
qa.answer: Properties include Argument of type String|Expression, which provides the text or expression to print to the console/host output.

qa.question: What does the summary describe?
qa.answer: The summary states that WriteHost prints messages to the console while a task runs so you can see what happened, making it easier to follow steps and spot problems during execution.

description: ### Purpose
The WriteHost plugin prints informational messages during automation execution for immediate visibility.
It takes a given argument and writes it to the console/host output so users can see what happened at each step.
This helps track progress and diagnose issues in real time.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature          | Description                                                              |
| Console Output   | Write the content of the `Argument` property to the host/console output. |
| Debugging Aid    | Print key events and details to help diagnose and fix issues.            |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case           | Description                                                                  |
| Execution Tracking | Print key points to see the flow of an automated task and confirm steps ran. |
| Debugging          | Print messages that help identify where and why a process failed.            |
| Status Output      | Print important data or status updates during automation.                    |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Use Case              | Description                                                                          |
| Test Execution Output | Print messages during test runs to understand test flow and state.                   |
| Debugging Tests       | Print details that help pinpoint failures or unexpected behavior in automated tests. |
| Recording Test Data   | Print test inputs, outputs, and results for later analysis and verification.         |

annotations.expected_result: The message "Writing a simple message" is printed to the console output.
annotations.notes: Host output is produced for visibility during execution.

description: ### Write a Static Console Message
This example demonstrates how to print a simple, static message to the console during automation execution.
It uses the `WriteHost` plugin with the argument set to "Writing a simple message".
Host output is produced for visibility during execution.

annotations.expected_result: The value of the session-scoped parameter "MyParam" is retrieved and printed to the console output.
annotations.notes: Host output is produced for visibility during execution.

description: ### Write a Dynamic Parameter Value to Console
This example demonstrates how to retrieve a dynamic, session-scoped parameter and print its value to the console.
It uses the `WriteHost` plugin with the argument set to `{{$Get-Parameter --Name:MyParam --Scope:Session}}`.
Host output is produced for visibility during execution.
Text or information to print to the console/host output.
It can be a fixed phrase or a value that changes at runtime.
Clear messages help track what the system does in real time.
properties.name: Argument

summary: `WriteHost` prints messages to the console while a task runs so you can see what happened.
It outputs your message to the host stream for immediate visibility during execution.
This makes it easy to follow steps, spot problems, and capture important notes in real time.
